question:
Blackburn Rovers FC vs. Ipswich Town FC: Both Teams to Score

Description: In the upcoming EFL Championship game between Blackburn Rovers FC and Ipswich Town FC, scheduled for December 2 at 2:45 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Blackburn Rovers FC and Ipswich Town FC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.efl.com/
event_title:
Blackburn Rovers FC vs. Ipswich Town FC: Both Teams to Score

Description: In the upcoming EFL Championship game between Blackburn Rovers FC and Ipswich Town FC, scheduled for December 2 at 2:45 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Blackburn Rovers FC and Ipswich Town FC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.efl.com/

evidences.title: Blackburn vs Ipswich: Predictions, odds, Lineups, Preview
evidences.content: Draw: 1-1 vs Watford (H) ## Head-to-Head Record The head-to-head record between Blackburn Rovers and Ipswich Town shows a competitive rivalry, with the most recent encounter resulting in a 0-1 win for Ipswich Town in March 2024. Before that, the teams played out a thrilling 4-3 victory for Ipswich in September 2023. However, Blackburn have had success in the past, with a 2-0 win in January 2019, and the two teams have also played out a 2-2 draw in August 2018. This suggests that matches between these two sides can often be close and unpredictable. Recent Championship Results Blackburn Rovers 0-1 Ipswich Town (29 Mar 2024) Ipswich Town 4-3 Blackburn Rovers (23 Sep 2023) Blackburn Rovers 2-0 Ipswich Town (19 Jan 2019) Ipswich Town 2-2 Blackburn Rovers (04 Aug 2018) [...] Bet 5 get 30 ### Ipswich Town Ipswich have been in decent form, with two wins, two draws, and one loss in their last five games. Their most recent result was a 1-2 loss to Oxford United away from home, but before that, they had a strong run of form, including a 2-0 win against Hull City and a 4-1 thrashing of Swansea. This should give them plenty of confidence heading into this match against Blackburn, and they'll...

evidences.content: 12.1 The Competition shall observe the League Regulations and The Football Association Rules and all Competition Matches shall be played under the Laws of the Game (as defined in The League Regulations). In the event of any conflict between any such Rules, Regulations, Bye-Laws and Laws as aforesaid, the Rules of the Football Association shall prevail. 12.2 In all Competition Matches the half-time interval shall be fifteen minutes. 12.3 All Competition Matches shall be of ninety minutes’ duration but any Competition Match which from any cause whatever falls short of 90 minutes’ duration may be ordered to count as a completed fixture or be replayed in full or in part as the Management Committee may in their absolute discretion determine. 12.4 In the event of the scores being level at [...] 20.6 Any Match which, for any cause whatsoever, falls short of 90 (ninety) minutes duration may be ordered to count as a completed fixture, or be replayed in full or in part as The League may, in its absolute discretion, determine. 20.7 Failure to fulfil any Match will constitute a breach of these Rules unless the Club can demonstrate that: 20.7.1 the dominant and effective cause of the non-ful...

evidences.title: Blackburn vs Ipswich: Predictions, odds, Lineups, Preview
evidences.content: Blackburn Rovers are expected to line up in a 3-4-1-2 formation, with Pears in goal and a back three of Miller, McLoughlin, and Pratt. This setup should provide a solid defensive base, while allowing wing-backs Alebiosu and Hedges to push forward and support the attack led by Gudjohnsen, Ohashi, and Morishita. ### Ipswich Town (4-2-3-1) Predicted lineup: Walton (GK), Furlong (DF), O'Shea (DF), Kipré (DF), Davis (DF), Matusiwa (MF), Taylor (MF), Egeli (MF), Núñez (MF), Philogene (MF), Hirst (AT) [...] Ipswich Town, on the other hand, have had a more consistent run of form, with a recent loss to Oxford United being the only blemish in their last five games. Their 4-2-3-1 setup should allow them to control the midfield with Matusiwa and Taylor, while players like Philogene and Egeli can provide creativity and goal threat. This match could be closely contested, with both teams having the potential to score, but Ipswich's recent form might give them a slight edge. ## Form Guide & Team Overview ### Blackburn Rovers [...] Ipswich Town are predicted to use a 4-2-3-1 formation, with Walton between the sticks and a back four of Davis, Kipré, Furlong, and O'Shea. evidences.title: Premier League predictions 2025-26: BBC Sport pundits pick their top four - BBC Sport
evidences.content: This time we've also asked AI's opinion, and included what Opta's 'Supercomputer' came up with too. The AI prediction was generated using Microsoft Copilot Chat - we simply asked the tool to 'predict the Premier League table for the 2025-26 season'. Opta's 'Supercomputer' is actually a complex algorithm that uses a model based on betting market odds and the sports analytics company's own 'Power Rankings', which are calculated using past results. It has simulated the outcome of all 380 Premier League games 10,000 times and has come to the same conclusion as 12 of our pundits - including Chris Sutton, who performs a similar calculation when he makes his predictions each week. [...] Rachel Brown-Finnis: People scoffed and were very cynical about the Club World Cup but winning it has given them some momentum, especially with the young players they have got. They are such a young team and are still learning, so you are going to see more fluctuations in form than with, say, Liverpool, but they will come into the season flying. I'd be shocked if they don't win at least one trophy, and you cannot dismiss them as title contenders. Ellen White: Chelsea put players on these massively long...
